programme.desc: A despondent man finds himself torn between killing himself and killing his co-workers. Before he can do either, he's pulled into The Outer Limits where there are hundreds of versions of him, each with a different life and a different character.

programme.title: The Revelations Of 'Becka Paulson
programme.desc: A woman's life changes dramatically when she accidentally shoots herself in the head which causes her to not only grow more intelligent, but also to realize how dull her life was before the photo in a picture frame started talking to her.

programme.title: Bodies Of Evidence
programme.desc: When an incident at a space station leaves three people dead, the captain of the ship returns to Earth to face murder charges. Is the captain a murderous madman? Or has he actually seen the face of evil floating in the zero-gravity of deep space?

programme.title: Feasibility Study
programme.desc: The residents of a suburban neighborhood have their lives turned upside down when a four block section of their subdivision is ripped from the earth by a mysterious force and transported to a distant planet. David McCallum and Laura Harris star.

programme.title: A Special Edition
programme.desc: Muckraking TV journalist Donald Rivers (Alan Thicke), host of "The Whole Truth," has the show of a lifetime: rock solid-proof that the government and private defense contractors have been engaging in secret genetic cloning.

programme.title: In The Zone
programme.desc: Using an experimental treatment, a scientist (Pat Morita) helps an aging athlete (Adrian Pasdar) end his career on a high note by accelerating his reflexes for one last match. But with victory comes side-effects...courtesy of The Outer Limits.

programme.title: Rite Of Passage
programme.desc: In a society controlled by aliens, a mother rebels when she is forced to give up her child to Mother, a wise alien who acts as a parent to all infants. Determined to get the child back, she embarks upon a dangerous quest that will change her world.

programme.title: Criminal Nature
programme.desc: When Genetic Engineering creates super-babies, some suffer from Genetic Rejection Syndrome (GRS), making them stronger and more deadly. A detective (Gary Cole) must stop his GRS son, a suspected killer, but must take a serum to become more like him.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: Hunters pay to hunt androids that have a chip that keeps them from harming humans. However, secretly planted data allows the androids to disable the chip, and the balance of power shifts between men and machines! Starring Ron White and Doug Savant.

programme.title: Movie The Perfect Mate
programme.desc: Hope (Brittany Lucio) is a control freak and a perfectionist and nothing would please her more than bringing the perfect Christian future husband home for the weekend to meet her father Reverend Adams (Lawrence Hilton-Jacobs), her mother Mrs. Adams (Jackee Harry), and sister Cherish (KD Aubert). On the day they're scheduled to leave she's dumped by her fiancé. That's when she enlists the help of her platonic male friend, Jesse (Chico Benymon), who is the epitome of everything ungodly, to impersonate her ex, the upcoming pastor. While there, he learns to appreciate family and faith in this hilarious Christian Romance Comedy.

programme.title: Neighborliness
programme.desc: Planting season ends in three weeks and Oliver has yet to get his land plowed. Meanwhile, Uncle Joe (Edgar Buchanan) needs a new shotgun before duck hunting season starts and schemes to solve both of their problems...without Oliver knowing it.

programme.title: Lisa, The Helpmate
programme.desc: When Oliver (Eddie Albert) sends a sample of his soil to the State Scientific College, a report comes back that his farm contains ingredients never before found in soil. Could his soil problems just be cosmetic? Eleanor Audley guest stars.

programme.title: You Can't Plug In A 2 With A 6
programme.desc: Relying on statistics provided by the Department of Agriculture, Oliver (Eddie Albert) decides to plant wheat. But when he goes to Drucker's to buy seed, he learns the other farmers have a unique way to predict the best crop�Mrs. Ziffel's lumbago.

programme.title: Don't Call Us, We'll Call You
programme.desc: After some wheeling and dealing and mending a riff between Mr. Kimball and his mother, Oliver (Eddie Albert) finally has a phone installed, but the Hooterville Phone Company installs it directly outside his bedroom window...on top of the phone pole.

programme.title: Parity Begins At Home
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) plants his entire 160 acres with wheat, only to hear from The Crabwell Corners Conservation and Stabilization Committee, a local farm organization, that he was only permitted to grow eight acres of wheat�because of Mr. Haney.

programme.title: Lisa Has A Calf
programme.desc: The cow that Oliver (Eddie Albert) bought from Mr. Haney is pregnant. Afraid that Haney will claim the new calf, Oliver and Eb decide to keep it secret...only to have the people of Hooterville think that it is Lisa (Eva Gabor) who is expecting!

programme.title: The Wedding Anniversary
programme.desc: It's the Douglases' wedding anniversary and, after initially pretending not to remember, Oliver (Eddie Albert) makes clear that he knows the date very well, he just can't remember exactly how many years he and Lisa (Eva Gabor) have been married.

programme.title: What Happened In Scranton
programme.desc: Lisa (Eva Gabor) is shocked that Hooterville has no beauty parlor, and the Ladies' Every-Other-Wednesday Afternoon-Discussion-Club nominates her to make it happen. Of course, Oliver's mother is happy to help, even if it means underhanded tactics.

programme.title: Give Me Land, Lots Of Land
programme.desc: When Oliver's neighbor, Mr. Watson, decides to do some traveling, Oliver (Eddie Albert) buys the farm which adjoins his property, but not the house on it. Lisa (Eva Gabor) misunderstands and thinks the beautiful farmhouse is their new home.

programme.title: I Didn't Raise My Husband To Be A Fireman
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) discovers the only requirement to join the Hooterville Valley Fire Department is that he must play a musical instrument. Meanwhile Lisa (Eva Gabor) tries her hand at cooking something besides "hotscakes" for breakfast.

programme.title: Lisa Bakes A Cake
programme.desc: Believing he will never get a moment's peace, Oliver (Eddie Albert) is annoyed when Lisa (Eva Gabor) puts a listing in the new phone book: "Oliver Douglas, Attorney at Law"�but it is a different story when no one calls for his services.

programme.title: The Price Of Apples
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert)'s apple crop is ready to be harvested, and he decides to take them to market himself and make a killing. Of course, getting to market before the price drops proves a little more difficult than Oliver anticipates.

programme.title: What's In A Name?
programme.desc: Ralph (Mary Grace Canfield) becomes enamored of Mr. Kimball (Alvy Moore), the county agent. However, though she feminizes herself and gets all gussied up, he refuses to go out with a girl named Ralph. Can Oliver solve the situation legally?

programme.title: The Day Of Decision
programme.desc: Six months is up! Now, Lisa (Eva Gabor) gets to decide whether she and Oliver (Eddie Albert) will remain on the farm or go back to New York and their Park Avenue lifestyle! Will this be the final straw for Green Acres?

programme.title: The Deputy
programme.desc: Sam Drucker (Frank Cady) is leaving on a much-needed two-week vacation and talks Oliver (Eddie Albert) into assuming his duties as deputy sheriff. But Oliver soon learns enforcing the law isn't quite as easy as it looks.

programme.title: The Ballad Of Molly Turgiss
programme.desc: Whatever you do, don't say her name and especially while playing checkers down at Drucker's! An old hag named Molly Turgiss (Renie Riano), who died twenty years before the Douglases (Eddie Albert, Eva Gabor) came to town, might be haunting them.

programme.title: Never Look A Gift Tractor In The Mouth
programme.desc: It's a few days before Oliver (Eddie Albert)'s birthday, and Lisa (Eva Gabor) doesn't know quite what to get him�until the old Hoyt Clagwell goes kerflooey! So, she goes out and buys the biggest and fanciest tractor she can find.

programme.title: Gomez, The Politician
programme.desc: Election fever grips the Addams household, as Gomez (John Astin) supports the council candidate whose campaign promise is to drain the town's disgusting bogs. Morticia (Carolyn Jones), who likes "the lovely bogs," labels the candidate an extremist.

programme.title: The Addams Family Tree
programme.desc: Pugsley and Wednesday start a row at a party when their gift of a tarantula upsets Harold, the birthday boy. Harold's parents insist that the Addams children lack breeding and, therefore, are no longer allowed to play with their son.

programme.title: Morticia Joins The Ladies League
programme.desc: After Pugsley befriends Gorgo, a circus gorilla, Gorgo decides to follow Pugsley and Gomez home, where he is accepted as one of the family. But Gorgo gives Morticia's tea guests, the Ladies League members, quite a shock.

programme.title: Halloween With The Addams Family
programme.desc: A pair of bank robbers are welcomed as Halloween trick-or-treaters by Morticia and Gomez. The creepy atmosphere of the house, Morticia's smoldering holiday punch and Lurch's ominous presence impel the crooks to abandon their plans.

programme.title: Green Eyed Gomez
programme.desc: When Lionel Barker, a former suitor of Morticia, shows up, Gomez is jealous. But he's soon calmed when he learns that Barker is after Mildred, the new maid. The Addamses then discover that Barker is a con man--after Mildred's money, not her love.

programme.title: New Neighbors Meet The Addams Family
programme.desc: Newlyweds move in next door. But the honeymoon loses its tranquility when Uncle Fester enters their house through a tunnel and they flee in terror at the sight of Lurch, Grandmama sharpening her ax, and Morticia and Gomez bathing the octopus.

programme.title: Wednesday Leaves Home
programme.desc: The family faces a crisis when Wednesday packs her spider and runs away (after being scolded for playing with Uncle Fester's TNT). When they try to file a missing person report, a detective is anxious to retire early due to their story.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets The V.I.P.'s
programme.desc: Official visitors from an unfriendly nation receive the shock of their lives when they decide to exploit the Addams Family as an average uncultured family. The visitors are shaken up by Morticia's carnivorous plants among other things.

programme.title: Morticia, The Matchmaker
programme.desc: Morticia's cousin, Melancholia, wants to marry Clarence P. Harvey after most of her marital prospects have joined the Foreign Legion. Clarence recoils when Melancholia puts on a campaign to win him, backed by Morticia, Gomez and Clarence's boss.

programme.title: Lurch Learns To Dance
programme.desc: Lurch becomes a veritable gazelle when he takes dancing lessons at Gomez and Morticia's suggestion, after he admits to being a wallflower at social functions. Each member of the Addams Family teaches Lurch a different step.

programme.title: Art And The Addams Family
programme.desc: Grandmama's paintings leave an art critic speechless, so Gomez sends for his friend, Sam Picasso, to teach Grandmama to paint in the abstract. The starving artist ignores Grandmama's and Morticia's work as he eats and paints.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets A Beatnik
programme.desc: Rockland Cartright III is a beatnik who crashes his motorbike into a tree and recuperates at the Addams house. Each regards the other as an oddity, although the family learns to appreciate certain aspects of Rockland's life.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets The Undercover Man
programme.desc: Mr. Briggs, the postman, is hired by the government to do undercover work in the Addams house. While pretending to be a stamp collector, he investigates rumors that coded radio messages are being sent from the house.

programme.title: Mother Lurch Visits The Addams Family
programme.desc: Lurch's tiny fire-eating mother visits and demands that Lurch's employers wait on her boy hand-and-foot after Lurch leads her to believe that it's his house. Lurch revels in the unexpected attention he gets from Morticia and Gomez.

programme.title: Uncle Fester's Illness
programme.desc: Uncle Fester's electrical output fails abruptly. Should they call a doctor or an electrician to bring Fester back? Morticia prescribes a bowl of porcupine mold, and both she an Gomez agree that long exposure to moon rays would be beneficial.

programme.title: The Addams Family Splurges
programme.desc: The family would like to vacation on the moon, but the billion dollar travel cost is slightly prohibitive. Uncle Fester volunteers $21.13 and Morticia, using Gomez's new supercomputer, suggests placing a $2,000 bet on the daily double horse race.

programme.title: Cousin Itt Visits The Addams Family
programme.desc: Cousin Itt is advocated as curator of the local zoo. Gomez uses his influence with Park Commissioner Fisk (guest star Alan Reed) to promote Itt for the job, but the zookeepers are convinced the Addamses' cousin should be confined to a cage.
